steps.title: Identify why retention needs bolstering
steps.content: The phrase “bolster retention” signals that something is weakening customer commitment. The sentence structure—”initial enthusiasm... quick to _____, and sellers must thus devise incentives”—shows causation: something happens fast, and that's why sellers need incentives. For this to make sense, initial enthusiasm must be disappearing.

steps.title: Match the word to the retention logic
steps.content: ”Subside” fits perfectly: when enthusiasm subsides quickly, customers naturally lose interest in their subscriptions, creating the turnover that makes retention challenging and necessitates seller incentives. The other choices either contradict the logic, describe irrelevant processes, or don't fit the meaning. That matches choice A — A is correct.
